tlg0003.tlg001.1st1K-eng1.mentioned_polities_in_work: Classical Athens; Classical Sparta and Peloponnesian League
tlg0003.tlg001.1st1K-eng1.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Thucydides narrates the 27-year war between the Athenian and Peloponnesian alliances.
tlg0003.tlg001.1st1K-eng1.mentioned_time_reasoning: Opens at the war's outbreak (-431) and ends mid-narrative at -411 where Thucydides stops; Thucydides served in the war.

tlg0004.tlg001.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: The work documents the lives and doctrines of major Greek philosophical schools across various poleis.
tlg0004.tlg001.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans from the Pre-Socratics (c. -600) to the early 3rd century CE, well before the author's likely floruit.

tlg0007.tlg054.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life of the Athenian orator Demosthenes and his opposition to Macedon.
tlg0007.tlg054.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the lifetime of Demosthenes from his birth in -384 to his death in -322.

tlg0007.tlg065.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polities_in_work: Roman Empire (Year of the Four Emperors)
tlg0007.tlg065.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the brief reign of the emperor Galba during the Roman civil wars of 68-69 CE.
tlg0007.tlg065.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the events of the Year of the Four Emperors (68-69 CE), which occurred shortly before Plutarch's birth or during his infancy.

tlg0007.tlg062.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Compares Dion of Syracuse with Marcus Junius Brutus, the assassin of Caesar.
tlg0007.tlg062.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans from the birth of Dion (c. -408) to the death of Brutus in -42.

tlg0007.tlg009.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Compares the Athenian lawgiver Solon with the early Roman consul Valerius Publicola.
tlg0007.tlg009.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans from Solon's birth (c. -638) to the death of Publicola (c. -503).

tlg0007.tlg036.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life of the Roman general Lucullus, focusing on his campaigns in the East.
tlg0007.tlg036.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the lifetime of Lucullus from approximately -118 to -57.

tlg0007.tlg038.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life of the Athenian politician and general Nicias during the Peloponnesian War.
tlg0007.tlg038.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the lifetime of Nicias from approximately -470 to his execution in Sicily in -413.

tlg0007.tlg007.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life and legislative reforms of the Athenian statesman Solon.
tlg0007.tlg007.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the lifetime of Solon from approximately -638 to -558.

tlg0007.tlg063.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life of Aratus of Sicyon, a leader of the Achaean League.
tlg0007.tlg063.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the lifetime of Aratus from -271 to -213.

tlg0007.tlg064.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life of the Persian King Artaxerxes II.
tlg0007.tlg064.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the lifetime of Artaxerxes II from approximately -436 to -358.

tlg0007.tlg001.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the legendary life and exploits of the Athenian hero Theseus.
tlg0007.tlg001.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Uses the traditional chronological anchor for the heroic age and the Trojan War era (c. -1300 to -1200).

tlg0007.tlg039.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life of Marcus Licinius Crassus, member of the First Triumvirate.
tlg0007.tlg039.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the lifetime of Crassus from approximately -115 to his death at Carrhae in -53.

tlg0007.tlg006.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Compares the Spartan lawgiver Lycurgus with the second Roman king, Numa Pompilius.
tlg0007.tlg006.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans from the traditional Lycurgan horizon (c. -900) to the death of Numa Pompilius in -673.

tlg0007.tlg030.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life of Pyrrhus of Epirus and his conflicts with Rome and Carthage.
tlg0007.tlg030.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the lifetime of Pyrrhus from -319 to -272.

tlg0007.tlg091.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A Pythian dialogue set in Plutarch's own time discussing the history and decline of the Delphic oracle.
tlg0007.tlg091.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Discusses the oracle's history from the Archaic period through to Plutarch's contemporary era (c. 110 CE).

tlg0007.tlg079.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A fictionalized symposium featuring the Seven Sages of Greece in the 6th century BCE.
tlg0007.tlg079.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Set during the lifetime of the Seven Sages, specifically around the early 6th century BCE.

tlg0007.tlg074.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A philosophical essay (De fortuna) using historical exempla from both Greek and Roman history.
tlg0007.tlg074.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Draws on historical examples from the Classical period through to the early Roman Empire.

tlg0007.tlg138.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A polemical work attacking Stoic doctrines, primarily referencing Hellenistic philosophical developments.
tlg0007.tlg138.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Focuses on the doctrines of Chrysippus and other early Stoics from the 3rd and 2nd centuries BCE.

tlg0007.tlg085.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A collection of short historical or mythical parallels (Parallela Minora) between Greek and Roman figures.
tlg0007.tlg085.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers legendary and historical events from the founding of Rome and early Greece to the end of the Republic.

tlg0007.tlg113.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: The Amatorius is a dialogue set in Plutarch's own time during a festival at Thespiae.
tlg0007.tlg113.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Set during Plutarch's adulthood in the late 1st century CE.

tlg0007.tlg092.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A dialogue set in Delphi during Plutarch's time discussing the historical decline of oracles.
tlg0007.tlg092.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: The dialogue is set in 83 CE but discusses the history of oracles dating back to the Classical period.

tlg0007.tlg108.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A philosophical treatise on the nature of fate, likely written for a contemporary audience.
tlg0007.tlg108.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: A philosophical essay reflecting the intellectual climate of the early 2nd century CE.

tlg0007.tlg127.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A scientific/philosophical inquiry into the nature of cold, addressed to a contemporary peer.
tlg0007.tlg127.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: A technical treatise written during Plutarch's floruit in the Roman Empire.

tlg0007.tlg134.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A commentary on Plato's Timaeus, focusing on the philosophical world of 4th-century BCE Athens.
tlg0007.tlg134.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Analyzes Plato's late dialogue, written in the mid-4th century BCE.

tlg0007.tlg136.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A critique of the internal inconsistencies in the writings of early Stoic philosophers.
tlg0007.tlg136.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Focuses on the works of Zeno, Cleanthes, and Chrysippus from the 3rd century BCE.

tlg0007.tlg096.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A moral essay written to a friend (Paccius) regarding mental well-being in the contemporary Roman world.
tlg0007.tlg096.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Written as a personal letter/treatise during the early 2nd century CE.

tlg0007.tlg076.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A letter of consolation to a contemporary individual, Apollonius, on the death of his son.
tlg0007.tlg076.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: A contemporary social and philosophical document from the Roman Imperial period.

tlg0007.tlg095.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A dialogue between contemporary characters (Fundanus and Sulla) on managing temper.
tlg0007.tlg095.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Features Plutarch's contemporaries in the Roman administrative and intellectual circles.

tlg0007.tlg025.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: The biography of Marcus Porcius Cato (Cato the Elder), the Roman statesman.
tlg0007.tlg025.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the lifetime of Cato the Elder from his birth in -234 to his death in -149.

tlg0007.tlg140.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: This polemical work targets Colotes, a disciple of Epicurus, focusing on the Epicurean school in the Hellenistic period.
tlg0007.tlg140.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: The work addresses the doctrines and life of Colotes and Epicurus, who flourished in the late 4th and early 3rd centuries BCE.

tlg0007.tlg032.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life of the Spartan admiral Lysander during the Peloponnesian War and its aftermath.
tlg0007.tlg032.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers Lysander's life from his early career through the end of the Peloponnesian War to his death in -395.

tlg0007.tlg035.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life of the Athenian statesman and general Cimon.
tlg0007.tlg035.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers Cimon's life from the aftermath of the Persian Wars to his death during the siege of Citium in -450.

tlg0007.tlg003.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A comparative study of the legendary founders of Athens and Rome.
tlg0007.tlg003.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans from the legendary era of Theseus (~13th century BCE) to the traditional death of Romulus in -717.

tlg0007.tlg004.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life and reforms of the legendary Spartan lawgiver Lycurgus.
tlg0007.tlg004.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Refers to the traditional 9th-century BCE horizon for the Lycurgan reforms in Sparta.

tlg0007.tlg050.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life of Marcus Porcius Cato Uticensis, a key figure in the late Republic.
tlg0007.tlg050.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers Cato's lifetime from his birth in -95 to his suicide at Utica in -46.

tlg0007.tlg057.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life of Demetrius Poliorcetes, a major Diadochus and King of Macedon.
tlg0007.tlg057.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the life of Demetrius from his birth to his death in captivity in -283.

tlg0007.tlg061.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life of Marcus Junius Brutus, the assassin of Julius Caesar.
tlg0007.tlg061.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers Brutus's life from his birth in -85 to his suicide after the Battle of Philippi in -42.

tlg0007.tlg066.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polities_in_work: Roman Empire (Year of the Four Emperors)
tlg0007.tlg066.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the brief reign of the Emperor Otho during the civil wars of 69 CE.
tlg0007.tlg066.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers Otho's life from birth in 32 CE to his suicide in 69 CE; Plutarch lived through this era.

tlg0007.tlg059.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Compares the Hellenistic king Demetrius Poliorcetes with the Roman triumvir Mark Antony.
tlg0007.tlg059.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans from the birth of Demetrius (-337) to the death of Mark Antony in -30.

tlg0007.tlg005.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life and religious reforms of Numa Pompilius, the second king of Rome.
tlg0007.tlg005.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the traditional dates of Numa's life and his 43-year reign ending in -673.

tlg0007.tlg002.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life of Romulus, the legendary founder and first king of Rome.
tlg0007.tlg002.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the traditional lifespan of Romulus from his birth to his disappearance/death in -717.

tlg0007.tlg034.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Compares the Spartan general Lysander with the Roman dictator Lucius Cornelius Sulla.
tlg0007.tlg034.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans from the birth of Lysander (~-454) to the death of Sulla in -78.

tlg0007.tlg033.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life and dictatorship of Lucius Cornelius Sulla.
tlg0007.tlg033.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers Sulla's life from his birth in -138 to his death in -78.

tlg0007.tlg067.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: An educational treatise reflecting the pedagogical values of the high Roman Empire.
tlg0007.tlg067.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Reflects the contemporary educational environment and social norms of Plutarch's own lifetime.

tlg0007.tlg103.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A moral essay using exempla from both the Greek past and the contemporary Roman world to discuss avarice.
tlg0007.tlg103.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Draws on historical anecdotes from the Classical period through Plutarch's own era (c. 100 CE).

tlg0007.tlg105.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A philosophical treatise comparing envy and hatred using historical and contemporary examples.
tlg0007.tlg105.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Uses exempla spanning from the Classical Greek era to the author's contemporary Roman context.

tlg0007.tlg114.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A collection of tragic and romantic tales primarily set in the Greek world of the past.
tlg0007.tlg114.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Narrates legendary and historical love stories from the Archaic through Hellenistic periods.

tlg0007.tlg075.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A short moral discourse contrasting virtue and vice through various historical anecdotes.
tlg0007.tlg075.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans from the Classical Greek period to the author's own time in the early Roman Empire.

tlg0007.tlg081.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polities_in_work: Achaemenid Empire; Classical Greek poleis; Macedonian Empire; Roman Republic; Roman Empire
tlg0007.tlg081.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A vast collection of apophthegms from diverse rulers and generals across Mediterranean history.
tlg0007.tlg081.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers figures from Cyrus the Great (-559) to the Emperor Augustus (14 CE).

tlg0007.tlg100.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A philosophical comparison of mental and physical suffering, largely contemporary in its ethical focus.
tlg0007.tlg100.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Reflects the philosophical and medical discourse of Plutarch's own lifetime.

tlg0007.tlg137.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A polemical work critiquing Stoic paradoxes relative to poetic myths.
tlg0007.tlg137.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Engages with Stoic doctrine from its Hellenistic origins through its Roman-era developments.

tlg0007.tlg126.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A dialogue on lunar physics and myth set among Plutarch's contemporaries.
tlg0007.tlg126.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: A contemporary dialogue reflecting the scientific and religious thought of the late 1st century CE.

tlg0007.tlg097.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: An essay on sibling relationships using historical examples like the Diadochi and Roman families.
tlg0007.tlg097.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Draws on historical examples from the Classical era to the author's contemporary Roman society.

tlg0007.tlg093.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A philosophical inquiry into the nature of virtue, heavily citing Classical Greek thinkers.
tlg0007.tlg093.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Focuses on the pedagogical debates of the Sophists and Socratic schools in the 5th-4th centuries BCE.

tlg0007.tlg088.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A declamation comparing the military and intellectual achievements of Athens.
tlg0007.tlg088.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Focuses on the golden age of Athens from the Persian Wars to the death of Demosthenes.

tlg0007.tlg130.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A humorous dialogue between Odysseus and one of Circe's pigs (Gryllus).
tlg0007.tlg130.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Set in the mythological timeframe of the aftermath of the Trojan War.

tlg0007.tlg119.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A political treatise comparing forms of government using historical and contemporary frameworks.
tlg0007.tlg119.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Analyzes Greek political theory and its application within the context of the Roman Empire.

tlg0007.tlg069.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Advice for young students on how to benefit from philosophical lectures in the Imperial era.
tlg0007.tlg069.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Addresses the educational practices and social environment of Plutarch's own time.

tlg0007.tlg117.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: An essay encouraging elderly men to remain active in civic life, citing many historical precedents.
tlg0007.tlg117.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Uses examples from Solon and Sophocles to contemporary Roman statesmen of the 1st century CE.

tlg0007.tlg087.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: These orations discuss the character and conquests of Alexander the Great across the Macedonian and Persian realms.
tlg0007.tlg087.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: The work focuses on the career and lifespan of Alexander the Great (-356 to -323).

tlg0007.tlg086.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polities_in_work: Early Roman Kingdom; Roman Republic; Roman Empire
tlg0007.tlg086.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Plutarch surveys the rise of Rome from its mythic origins through the early Principate to argue for the role of Fortune.
tlg0007.tlg086.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the span from Rome's traditional founding (-753) to the end of the Flavian dynasty (96 CE).

tlg0007.tlg123.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Plutarch critiques Herodotus's account of the Persian Wars and the Greek city-states involved.
tlg0007.tlg123.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: The critique focuses on Herodotus's narrative of the 6th and 5th centuries BCE, specifically the Persian Wars.

tlg0007.tlg139.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A polemic against Epicureanism, documenting the doctrines and lives of Epicurus and his followers in Athens.
tlg0007.tlg139.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans from the birth of Epicurus (-341) to the contemporary philosophical debates in Plutarch's time.

tlg0007.tlg132.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A philosophical treatise drawing on Pythagorean traditions and Greek/Roman dietary customs.
tlg0007.tlg132.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: References Pythagorean origins (-570) and continues through to Plutarch's contemporary ethical context.

tlg0007.tlg016.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life of the Roman general Coriolanus and his conflict with the Roman plebs and alliance with the Volscians.
tlg0007.tlg016.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the traditional dates of Coriolanus's life and the secession of the plebs in the early 5th century BCE.

tlg0007.tlg131.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A philosophical treatise drawing on Pythagorean traditions and Greek/Roman dietary customs.
tlg0007.tlg131.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: References Pythagorean origins (-570) and continues through to Plutarch's contemporary ethical context.

tlg0007.tlg109.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A dialogue set during the liberation of Thebes in 379 BCE, discussing Socrates's 'daemon'.
tlg0007.tlg109.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: The dialogue is set in -379 but centers on the legacy of Socrates (died -399).

tlg0007.tlg129.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A dialogue comparing animal intelligence, using examples from across the Greco-Roman world.
tlg0007.tlg129.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: The dialogue is set among Plutarch's contemporaries and uses contemporary anecdotes.

tlg0007.tlg121.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Biographies of the ten canonical Attic orators who shaped Athenian political life.
tlg0007.tlg121.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the lives of orators from Antiphon to Dinarchus, spanning the 5th and 4th centuries BCE.

tlg0007.tlg071.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: An ethical essay addressed to a contemporary Roman friend (Sosius Senecio) regarding moral self-improvement.
tlg0007.tlg071.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Reflects the social and philosophical environment of Plutarch's own era in the late 1st/early 2nd century CE.

tlg0007.tlg122.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A literary comparison between the Old Comedy of Aristophanes and the New Comedy of Menander.
tlg0007.tlg122.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans from the career of Aristophanes (mid-5th c. BCE) to the death of Menander (early 3rd c. BCE).

tlg0007.tlg094.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A philosophical essay on ethics, drawing on the traditions of the Academy and Peripatetics.
tlg0007.tlg094.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Draws on Platonic and Aristotelian foundations (-380) applied to Plutarch's contemporary Roman context.

tlg0007.tlg084b.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polities_in_work: Archaic Greek poleis; Classical Greek poleis
tlg0007.tlg084b.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: An antiquarian work explaining the origins of obscure customs and names across various Greek cities.
tlg0007.tlg084b.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Focuses on the origins of customs from the Archaic period through the Classical era.

tlg0007.tlg106.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: An ethical treatise on social conduct, using historical examples from Greece and Rome to advise contemporaries.
tlg0007.tlg106.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Uses historical exempla from the 5th century BCE onwards to address contemporary Roman social etiquette.

tlg0007.tlg008.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: The work documents the life of Publius Valerius Publicola, a key figure in the founding of the Roman Republic.
tlg0007.tlg008.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the lifetime of Publicola, from his early years to his death in -503 BCE.

tlg0007.tlg037.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Compares the Roman general Lucullus with the Athenian statesman Cimon.
tlg0007.tlg037.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans from Cimon's birth (~-510) to Lucullus's death in -56 BCE.

tlg0007.tlg015.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polities_in_work: Classical Athens; Classical Sparta; Achaemenid Empire
tlg0007.tlg015.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life of Alcibiades and his shifting allegiances during the Peloponnesian War.
tlg0007.tlg015.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the life of Alcibiades from his birth (~-450) to his assassination in -404 BCE.

tlg0007.tlg012.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Focuses on the life and leadership of Pericles during the Athenian Golden Age.
tlg0007.tlg012.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the lifetime of Pericles, from roughly -495 to his death in -429 BCE.

tlg0007.tlg024.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Narrates the life of Aristides the Just, an Athenian statesman and general.
tlg0007.tlg024.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the life of Aristides from his birth (~-530) to his death in -468 BCE.

tlg0007.tlg023.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Compares the Theban general Pelopidas with the Roman general Marcellus.
tlg0007.tlg023.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans from the birth of Pelopidas (~-410) to the death of Marcellus in -208 BCE.

tlg0007.tlg048.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life, military campaigns, and dictatorship of Julius Caesar.
tlg0007.tlg048.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers Caesar's lifetime from his birth in -100 to his assassination in -44 BCE.

tlg0007.tlg046.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Compares the Spartan king Agesilaus II with the Roman general Pompey the Great.
tlg0007.tlg046.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans from the birth of Agesilaus (~-444) to the death of Pompey in -48 BCE.

tlg0007.tlg041.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life of Eumenes of Cardia, a secretary and general under Alexander and his successors.
tlg0007.tlg041.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the life of Eumenes from his birth (~-362) to his execution in -316 BCE.

tlg0007.tlg022.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Focuses on Marcus Claudius Marcellus, a Roman general during the Second Punic War.
tlg0007.tlg022.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the life of Marcellus from his birth (~-268) to his death in battle in -208 BCE.

tlg0007.tlg013.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Narrates the life of Fabius Maximus, known for his 'Fabian strategy' against Hannibal.
tlg0007.tlg013.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the life of Fabius Maximus from his birth (~-280) to his death in -203 BCE.

tlg0007.tlg014.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Compares the Athenian statesman Pericles with the Roman general Fabius Maximus.
tlg0007.tlg014.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans from the birth of Pericles (~-495) to the death of Fabius Maximus in -203 BCE.

tlg0007.tlg040.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Compares the Athenian general Nicias with the Roman triumvir Marcus Licinius Crassus.
tlg0007.tlg040.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans from the birth of Nicias (~-470) to the death of Crassus in -53 BCE.

tlg0007.tlg047.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life and conquests of Alexander the Great.
tlg0007.tlg047.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers Alexander's lifetime from his birth in -356 to his death in -323 BCE.

tlg0007.tlg049.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Narrates the life of Phocion, an Athenian statesman and general during the rise of Macedon.
tlg0007.tlg049.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the life of Phocion from his birth (~-402) to his execution in -318 BCE.

tlg0007.tlg060.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life of Dion of Syracuse and his political struggles against the tyrants Dionysius I and II.
tlg0007.tlg060.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans Dion's life from approximately -408 to his assassination in -354.

tlg0007.tlg051.perseus-eng1.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Focuses on the Spartan king Agis IV and his attempts to reform the Spartan constitution.
tlg0007.tlg051.perseus-eng1.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the life and reign of Agis IV, ending with his execution in -241.

tlg0007.tlg080.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A philosophical essay drawing on various Greek and Roman cultural and religious examples to discuss superstition.
tlg0007.tlg080.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Draws on historical exempla from the Archaic period through to Plutarch's own contemporary era.

tlg0007.tlg089.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Discusses Egyptian mythology and its interpretation within the Hellenistic and Roman religious context.
tlg0007.tlg089.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans ancient Egyptian mythic origins to Plutarch's own philosophical reception in the 1st century CE.

tlg0007.tlg042.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life of Quintus Sertorius and his independent regime in Hispania against the Sullan Roman government.
tlg0007.tlg042.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers Sertorius's life from his birth to his assassination in -72.

tlg0007.tlg045.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Narrates the career of Pompey the Great, including his Roman political life and his eastern conquests.
tlg0007.tlg045.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans Pompey's birth in -106 to his murder in Egypt in -48.

tlg0007.tlg011.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life of Marcus Furius Camillus, the 'Second Founder of Rome', during the Gallic invasions.
tlg0007.tlg011.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the traditional dates of Camillus's life and his multiple dictatorships in the 4th century BCE.

tlg0007.tlg029.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Compares the Greek general Philopoemen with the Roman general Titus Flamininus.
tlg0007.tlg029.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the overlapping careers of both men during the Roman intervention in Greece, roughly -253 to -174.

tlg0007.tlg020.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Compares the Greek liberator Timoleon with the Roman general Aemilius Paullus.
tlg0007.tlg020.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans from the birth of Timoleon to the death of Aemilius Paullus in -160.

tlg0007.tlg027.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life of Philopoemen, the 'Last of the Greeks', and his leadership of the Achaean League.
tlg0007.tlg027.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers Philopoemen's life from -253 to his death by poisoning in -183.

tlg0007.tlg018.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Narrates Timoleon's expedition from Corinth to liberate Syracuse from tyrants and Carthaginians.
tlg0007.tlg018.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers Timoleon's life and his Sicilian campaign, ending with his death in -337.

tlg0007.tlg044.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life and reign of the Spartan king Agesilaus II during Sparta's period of hegemony.
tlg0007.tlg044.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans the long life of Agesilaus II from approximately -444 to -360.

tlg0007.tlg043.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Compares the Roman rebel Sertorius with the Greek Diadochus Eumenes of Cardia.
tlg0007.tlg043.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans from the birth of Eumenes (~-362) to the death of Sertorius (-72).

tlg0007.tlg021.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life of the Theban general Pelopidas and his role in the liberation of Thebes and the Sacred Band.
tlg0007.tlg021.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers Pelopidas's career from the late 5th century to his death at the Battle of Cynoscephalae in -364.

tlg0551.tlg014.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the period from the outbreak of the First Mithridatic War in -89 to the death of Mithridates in -63.

tlg0551.tlg003.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the expansion of Roman power across the Italian peninsula and conflicts with various Italic tribes.
tlg0551.tlg003.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the early Republic's conquest of Italy, roughly from the expulsion of the kings to the start of the Punic Wars.

tlg0551.tlg004.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the three Samnite Wars spanning from -343 to the final Roman victory around -290.

tlg0551.tlg005.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Narrates the various Roman conflicts with Gauls, from the early incursions to Caesar's conquests.
tlg0551.tlg005.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Starts with the Gallic sack of Rome (-390) and extends through the final subjugation of Gaul by Julius Caesar.

tlg0551.tlg002.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the legendary and semi-historical period of the seven kings of Rome.
tlg0551.tlg002.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the traditional dates of the Roman regal period from the founding to the expulsion of Tarquinius Superbus.

tlg0551.tlg011.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Details the four Macedonian Wars and the eventual annexation of Macedon as a Roman province.
tlg0551.tlg011.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans from the First Macedonian War (-214) to the final settlement and destruction of Corinth in -146.

tlg0551.tlg017.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Focuses on the internal Roman conflicts from the Gracchi to the end of the Republic.
tlg0551.tlg017.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Starts with the tribunate of Tiberius Gracchus (-133) and ends with the death of Sextus Pompey (-35).

tlg0551.tlg010.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents Roman interactions with Numidia, particularly the Jugurthine War.
tlg0551.tlg010.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the period from the alliance in the Second Punic War to the suicide of Juba I in -46.

tlg0032.tlg015.perseus-eng1.mentioned_polity_reasoning: The work (often attributed to 'Old Oligarch') analyzes the political system and naval democracy of Athens.
tlg0032.tlg015.perseus-eng1.mentioned_time_reasoning: Internal evidence suggests it was written during the early years of the Peloponnesian War, roughly -431 to -424.

tlg0007.tlg098.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A moral essay drawing on universal human nature and exempla from both Greek history and the contemporary Roman world.
tlg0007.tlg098.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Draws on historical anecdotes from the Classical period through Plutarch's own era (c. 100 CE).

tlg0007.tlg107.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A philosophical dialogue discussing divine justice using historical examples from various Greek and Roman contexts.
tlg0007.tlg107.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Uses examples of divine retribution spanning from the Archaic Greek period to the author's contemporary Roman world.

tlg0007.tlg102.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A social critique of curiosity and meddling, referencing both contemporary Roman social life and Greek historical figures.
tlg0007.tlg102.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Combines timeless moral advice with historical anecdotes and contemporary social observations up to c. 100 CE.

tlg0007.tlg073.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A moral treatise on friendship using exempla from the Greek past and the contemporary Roman social environment.
tlg0007.tlg073.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Draws on historical precedents and contemporary ethics from the Classical era to the author's lifetime.

tlg0007.tlg072.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A practical ethical guide referencing historical figures like Xenophon and contemporary Roman political life.
tlg0007.tlg072.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Utilizes historical examples from the 5th century BCE through the author's own time in the early 2nd century CE.

tlg0007.tlg101.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A treatise on loquacity featuring anecdotes from across Greek and Roman history.
tlg0007.tlg101.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans from early Greek historical anecdotes to the author's contemporary Roman context.

tlg0007.tlg120.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: An essay against debt and usury, reflecting the economic realities of the Roman Empire and citing Greek precedents.
tlg0007.tlg120.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Addresses contemporary Roman economic issues while citing historical Greek examples.

tlg0007.tlg077.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A health manual for intellectuals in the Roman Empire, drawing on Greek medical and philosophical traditions.
tlg0007.tlg077.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Combines Classical Greek medical wisdom with the lifestyle of the contemporary Roman elite.

tlg0032.tlg009.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the full life and reign of Agesilaus II (-444 to -360), a contemporary of Xenophon.

tlg0032.tlg007.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A fictionalized biography of Cyrus the Great, the founder of the Persian Empire.
tlg0032.tlg007.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Documents the life and reign of Cyrus the Great in the 6th century BCE.

tlg0032.tlg006.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polities_in_work: Achaemenid Persia; Classical Greek mercenary polis-world
tlg0032.tlg006.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: An eyewitness account of the expedition of Cyrus the Younger and the retreat of the Ten Thousand Greek mercenaries.

tlg0032.tlg008.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A dialogue between the poet Simonides and the tyrant Hiero I of Syracuse regarding the nature of power.

tlg0032.tlg013.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A technical manual on horsemanship for the Greek cavalry class.

tlg0032.tlg012.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Written as a contemporary guide for military duties in the mid-4th century BCE (approx. 365 BCE).

tlg0099.tlg001.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polities_in_work: Roman Empire; Hellenistic Mediterranean; Italy; Greece; Asia Minor
tlg0099.tlg001.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Books 6-14 cover Italy, Sicily, Northern and Eastern Europe, Greece, and Asia Minor under Roman hegemony.

tlg0526.tlg002.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers Josephus's life from his birth in 37 CE to the end of the 1st century CE.

tlg0526.tlg004.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Focuses on the active conflict between the Jews and Romans from 66 CE to the fall of Masada in 74 CE.

tlg0551.tlg007.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the Roman conquest of the Iberian Peninsula and the resistance of local tribes.

tlg0551.tlg001.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: The preface summarizes Roman history from its founding (-753) to Appian's own time in the 2nd century CE.

tlg0551.tlg012.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Describes the Roman campaigns in Illyria and the eventual formation of the province.

tlg0551.tlg013.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Focuses on the Roman-Seleucid War, from the invasion of Greece (-192) to the Treaty of Apamea (-188).

tlg0007.tlg070.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A moral essay addressed to a Roman friend (Philopappus) using Greek philosophical and historical examples.
tlg0007.tlg070.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Combines contemporary social advice for the Roman elite with historical exempla from the Greek past.

tlg0007.tlg082.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A collection of apophthegms specifically documenting the speech and customs of Spartan men.

tlg0007.tlg141.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A polemic against the Epicurean maxim, focusing on Greek philosophical schools.
tlg0007.tlg141.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Engages with Epicurean thought (founded c. -306) and its reception up to Plutarch's own era.

tlg0007.tlg118.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Discusses contemporary local governance in the Roman Empire while citing classical Athenian and Spartan precedents.

tlg0007.tlg099.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Utilizes classical literary and philosophical examples to argue a perennial ethical point.

tlg0007.tlg078.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Practical advice for a newly married couple in Plutarch's circle, utilizing Greek and Roman customs.

tlg0007.tlg112.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Table-talk conversations among Plutarch's contemporaries, discussing science, history, and literature.
tlg0007.tlg112.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Records symposia and intellectual life during Plutarch's own career in the Roman Empire.

tlg0007.tlg068.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: An educational treatise on how to interpret classical Greek poets like Homer and Hesiod.

tlg0007.tlg116.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A discourse on the responsibilities of power, likely addressed to a Roman official or local magistrate.

tlg0007.tlg128.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A rhetorical exercise (declamatio) comparing the utility of elements within a Greek scientific framework.
tlg0007.tlg128.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Engages with long-standing Greek natural philosophy and rhetorical traditions.

tlg0007.tlg104.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A moral essay on overcoming excessive shyness, using historical anecdotes from Greece and Rome.
tlg0007.tlg104.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Combines contemporary psychological advice with historical examples of public figures.

tlg0007.tlg082b.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A collection of sayings attributed to Spartan women, documenting their unique social status and laconic wit.
tlg0007.tlg082b.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Documents the traditional sayings of Spartan women from the legendary past through the Hellenistic era.

tlg0007.tlg115.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Argues that philosophers should advise political leaders, reflecting Plutarch's own role in the Roman Empire.
tlg0007.tlg115.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Addresses the contemporary relationship between intellectuals and Roman power, citing Socratic and Platonic precedents.

tlg0007.tlg017.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: The work compares the Athenian statesman Alcibiades with the Roman general Coriolanus.
tlg0007.tlg017.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the lifetimes of Coriolanus (early 5th c. BCE) and Alcibiades (late 5th c. BCE).

tlg0007.tlg028.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life of the Roman general who defeated Philip V of Macedon.

tlg0007.tlg010.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Narrates the life of the Athenian leader during the Persian Wars and his later exile in Persia.
tlg0007.tlg010.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the lifetime of Themistocles, including the Battle of Salamis (-480).

tlg0007.tlg082a.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: An antiquarian collection of Spartan customs, laws, and educational practices.

tlg0007.tlg052.perseus-eng1.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the political careers and land reforms of the Gracchi brothers in Rome.
tlg0007.tlg052.perseus-eng1.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the period from the birth of Tiberius Gracchus to the death of Gaius Gracchus.

tlg0007.tlg090.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A philosophical dialogue set at the Temple of Apollo in Delphi during Plutarch's own time.
tlg0007.tlg090.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: The dialogue features Plutarch and his contemporaries discussing Delphic mysteries in the late 1st century CE.

tlg0007.tlg026.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Compares the Athenian statesman Aristides with the Roman censor Cato the Elder.
tlg0007.tlg026.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans from the birth of Aristides (c. -530) to the death of Cato the Elder (-149).

tlg0007.tlg019.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: Documents the life of the Roman general who conquered Macedon at the Battle of Pydna.
tlg0007.tlg019.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the lifetime of Lucius Aemilius Paullus Macedonicus.

tlg0007.tlg053.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: The work compares the Spartan kings Agis IV and Cleomenes III with the Roman reformers Tiberius and Gaius Gracchus.

tlg0007.tlg133.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: The work analyzes the philosophical doctrines and texts of Plato, set in the context of Classical Athenian thought.
tlg0007.tlg133.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Focuses on the life and writings of Plato, spanning his lifetime from approximately -427 to -347.

tlg0007.tlg056.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: This comparison pairs the Athenian orator Demosthenes with the Roman statesman and orator Cicero.
tlg0007.tlg056.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Spans from the birth of Demosthenes (-384) to the death of Cicero (-43).

tlg0007.tlg084a.perseus-eng3.mentioned_polity_reasoning: An antiquarian investigation into the origins of Roman customs, rituals, and social institutions.
tlg0007.tlg084a.perseus-eng3.mentioned_time_reasoning: Traces Roman customs from the traditional founding of the city (-753) up to Plutarch's own era (c. 100).

tlg0007.tlg111.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A personal letter written by Plutarch to his wife Timoxena regarding the death of their daughter.
tlg0007.tlg111.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Reflects a specific personal event during Plutarch's mature life in the Roman Empire, roughly around 90-110 CE.

tlg0007.tlg125.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A collection of scientific and natural inquiries reflecting the intellectual culture of the High Roman Empire.
tlg0007.tlg125.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Documents natural phenomena and scientific theories as understood and discussed during Plutarch's lifetime.

tlg0007.tlg031.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: A biography of the Roman general and seven-time consul Gaius Marius.
tlg0007.tlg031.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: Covers the lifetime of Gaius Marius, from his birth in -157 to his death in -86.

tlg0061.tlg004.perseus-eng1.mentioned_polity_reasoning: The dialogue focuses on the myth of Alcyone and Ceyx, set in a legendary Greek past.
tlg0061.tlg004.perseus-eng1.mentioned_time_reasoning: The work discusses mythological transformations from the heroic age, traditionally anchored around the 13th century BCE.

tlg0061.tlg001.perseus-eng1.mentioned_polity_reasoning: The novel follows the travels of Lucius through the Roman provinces of Achaia and Macedonia.
tlg0061.tlg001.perseus-eng1.mentioned_time_reasoning: The narrative reflects the social and administrative reality of the mid-2nd century CE Roman Empire.

tlg0061.tlg003.perseus-eng1.mentioned_polity_reasoning: The encomium celebrates the life of the orator Demosthenes and his resistance against Philip II and Alexander.
tlg0061.tlg003.perseus-eng1.mentioned_time_reasoning: The work covers the lifespan of Demosthenes, from his birth in -384 to his death in -322.

tlg0061.tlg006.perseus-eng1.mentioned_polity_reasoning: The dialogue features a Cynic philosopher defending his lifestyle within the context of the 2nd-century CE Greek-speaking Roman world.
tlg0061.tlg006.perseus-eng1.mentioned_time_reasoning: The work depicts a philosophical debate typical of the Second Sophistic era in which the author lived.

tlg0094.tlg002.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: The treatise documents the history of Greek music, citing figures from Sparta, Athens, and various Greek colonies.
tlg0094.tlg002.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: The work surveys the development of musical modes and poets from the 7th century BCE down to the late Classical period.

tlg0094.tlg003.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: This doxography records the opinions of various Greek philosophers from the Ionians to the Hellenistic schools.
tlg0094.tlg003.perseus-eng2.mentioned_time_reasoning: The work spans the history of Greek natural philosophy from Thales (-600) to the late Hellenistic period.

tlg0094.tlg001.perseus-eng2.mentioned_polity_reasoning: The work lists geographical features and the myths associated with them across the known world.
